aerie_protector.name: Aerie Protector
description: ## Aerie Protector
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/47›
The aerie protector guards nature's nests and lairs in the highest mountains, and she is only ever truly at ease when under vast expanses of open sky.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="Aerie protectors are proficient with ‹eq-weapon/javelin«s› and ‹eq-weapon/shortbow«s› but not with scimitars or scythes. Aerie protectors are not proficient with medium armor." alter="Weapon and armor proficiency"}
:::ab{title="Nature Bond" icon=info action=Choice alter="Nature bond"}

If the aerie protector chooses an animal companion as her nature bond, she must choose an animal with a fly speed. She can use spells or other effects to alter her companion's fly speed, but it must be able to fly on its own before it becomes her companion.
If the aerie protector chooses a domain as her nature bond, she must choose the ‹domain/Air›, ‹druiddomain/Eagle›, ‹domain/Earth›, ‹druiddomain/Mountain›, or ‹domain/Weather› domain.

::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost-down passive="An aerie protector gains a +4 bonus on *wild empathy* checks on flying animals, but she takes a -4 penalty on checks on other animals." alter="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Sky and Stone (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="An aerie protector gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› her level on Knowledge (geography) and Survival checks involving mountainous terrain or high altitudes, and she cannot be tracked in such an environment." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Wind at Her Back (Su)]{l=4 icon=def passive="An aerie protector gains a +4 bonus to CMD and on saving throws against the effects of high altitude and effects that rely on air pressure or strong wind to move her or impede her flight. This bonus also applies on Reflex saves against effects that deal electricity damage." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Wild Shape]{icon=down-boost l=6 ability="An aerie protector gains *wild shape,* except her effective druid level is her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of a flying animal, she instead uses her druid level + 2." alter="Wild shape"}

::ab[In the Wind (Ex)]{icon=boost l=9 passive="The climb and fly speeds of forms the aerie protector assumes with her *wild shape* ability (if any) increase by 10 feet. The maneuverability of her flying forms improves by one category." replace="Venom immunity"}

ancient_guardian.name: Ancient Guardian
description: ## Ancient Guardian
‹SOURCE Monster Codex/140›

Ancient guardians revere nature and draw power from its divine energies, but also protect the customs, histories, and lore of their tribe and race. They act as ambassadors and protectors of the old ways, nurturing their tribe's growth and holding back civilization's corruptive influence.

::ab[Ancient Arms]{icon=down-warning note="An ancient guardian can't wield weapons made primarily of metal. She is proficient with only the ‹eq-weapon/club›, ‹eq-weapon/dart›, ‹eq-weapon/quarterstaff›, ‹eq-weapon/shortspear›, ‹eq-weapon/sling›, and ‹eq-weapon/spear›. She is also proficient with all natural attacks (claw, bite, and so forth) of any form she assumes with *wild shape.* Using a prohibited weapon renders her unable to cast druid spells or use any of her druid supernatural or spell-like class abilities while wielding the weapon and for 24 hours after ceasing to wield it." alter="Weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Community Bond (Ex)]{icon=info choice="An ancient guardian must choose the domain nature bond ability and select from the following domains: ‹domain/Community›, ‹domain/Healing›, ‹domain/Knowledge›, ‹domain/Protection›, or ‹domain/Repose›." alter="Nature bond"}

::ab[Patience of Nature (Ex)]{icon=skill-boost l2="An ancient guardian adds ‹skill/Diplomacy› (CHA), ‹skill/Perform« (oratory)› (CHA), and ‹skill/Sense Motive› (WIS) to her list of class skills and gains an insight bonus equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level on such checks." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Ancient Ways (Ex)]{icon=boost l=3 passive="An ancient guardian increases any climb, fly, or swim speeds she has or gains via her *wild shape* ability by 10 feet." replace="Trackless step~woodland stride"}

::ab[Dispel Hostility (Su)]{icon=down-power l=3 ability="An ancient guardian can channel stored spell energy into a spell capable of dispelling hostile thoughts and strong emotions. She can &quot&lose&quot& a prepared spell in order to cast ‹spell/calm emotions›. Instead of the normal Will saving throw for that spell, the DC of a *calm emotions* spell cast with this ability is 10 + 1/2 the ancient guardian's druid level + her Wisdom modifier." replace="Spontaneous casting"}
::ab[Unimpeachable (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="An ancient guardian gains a +4 bonus on saves against enchantments." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Undo Artifice (Sp)]{l=13 icon=power-magic ability="An ancient guardian gains the ability to reduce a nonliving item to its component raw materials. This acts as the spell ‹spell/polymorph any object›, but works on magical or nonmagical nonliving objects. The duration is always permanent. Unlike *polymorph any object,* this ability can't be used to mimic the effects of other spells. Items subject to this effect receive a Fortitude saving throw to negate the effect (DC = 10 + 1/2 the ancient guardian's druid level + her Wisdom modifier). Magic items receive a circumstance bonus on this save equal to +1 for every 5,000 gp of the item's value. An ancient guardian can use *undo artifice* a number of times per day equal to her Wisdom modifier." replace="A thousand faces"}

ape_shaman.name: Ape Shaman
description: ## Ape Shaman
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/42›

A shaman with this totem calls upon the mighty ape, a peaceful but powerful simian whose strength is beyond compare. An ape shaman is a friendly protector of the forest, but will crush those enemies who rouse her anger.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="An ape shaman who chooses an animal companion must select an ‹companion/ape› or related primate. If choosing a domain, the ape shaman must choose from the ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Community› (Family subdomain), ‹domain/Destruction› (Rage subdomain), and ‹domain/Strength› domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="An ape shaman can use *wild empathy* with apes and other primates as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
An ape shaman may adopt an aspect of the ape while retaining her normal form. The druid gains one of the following sets of bonuses and abilities:
- **Movement** (climb speed 20 ft., +4 racial bonus on Climb checks)
- **Senses** (low-light vision, ‹umr/scent›)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (2 slams [1d6 for a Medium shaman], +2 on combat maneuver checks to ‹rule/grapple›)
- **Toughness** (+2 natural armor bonus to AC, ‹feat/Endurance› feat)
While using *totem transformation,* the ape shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (primates only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost-magic standard="An ape shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon primates, and these summoned creatures gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to any primate to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates." replace="A thousand faces"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost ability="An ape shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of an ape, she instead uses her druid level + 2." alter="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="An ape shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Diehard›, ‹feat/Endurance›, ‹feat/Great Fortitude›, ‹feat/Improved Bull Rush›, and ‹feat/Toughness›." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." imp13="The ape shaman gains another bonus feat from the list above." imp17="The ape shaman gains a third bonus feat from the list above." replace="Venom immunity"}

aquatic_druid.name: Aquatic Druid
description: ## Aquatic Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/98›
Shepherds of the lakes and seas, aquatic druids guard ecosystems ranging from shallows streams to deep ocean trenches, ministering to their residents and communing with the tides.

::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=down-boost ability="An aquatic druid's *wild empathy* functions only on creatures that have a swim speed or the aquatic or water subtype; however, she can improve the attitude of any such creature with Intelligence 2 or less regardless of type, including mindless creatures." alter="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Aquatic Adaptation (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-power passive="An aquatic druid gains an insight bonus on Initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, Survival, and Swim checks equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level in aquatic terrain, and she cannot be tracked such environments." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Natural Swimmer (Ex)]{icon=power l=3 ability="An aquatic druid gains a swim speed equal to half her land speed." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Resist Ocean's Fury (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="An aquatic druid gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against spells of the water type or the exceptional or supernatural abilities of creatures with the aquatic or water subtype." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down ability="An aquatic druid gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for the ability is equal to her druid level - 2." alter="Wild shape"}

::ab[Seaborn (Ex)]{icon=power-def l=9 passive="An aquatic druid gains the ‹subtype/aquatic› subtype, the ‹umr/amphibious› trait, and a swim speed equal to her land speed. She also can endure cold climate effects as if using ‹spell/endure elements›." replace="Venom immunity"}

::ab[Deep Diver (Ex)]{icon=def l=13 passive="A aquatic druid gains DR/slashing or piercing equal to 1/2 her level. This damage reduction also applies against spells and spell-like abilities that inflict damage by grappling or crushing (e.g., ‹spell/black tentacles›, ‹spell/crushing hand›). She never takes ‹rule/pressure damage› from deep water." replace="A thousand faces"}

arctic_druid.name: Arctic Druid
description: ## Arctic Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/98›
An arctic druid watches over the stark landscape of the far frozen reaches of the world, tending the stunted and rugged life that ekes out its survival in the least habitable climes.

::ab[Arctic Native (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-power passive="An arctic druid gains a bonus on Initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level in cold or icy terrain, and she cannot be tracked in cold or icy terrain." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Icewalking (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="An arctic druid suffers no penalty to speed or on Acrobatics, Climb, or Stealth checks in snowy or icy terrain or weather conditions and can walk across snow crusts or thin ice without breaking through." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Arctic Endurance (Ex)]{icon=def l=4 passive="An arctic druid ignores the effects of a cold climate as if using ‹spell/endure elements›. She is also immune to being dazzled." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{icon=down l=6 ability="An arctic druid gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for this ability is equal to her druid level - 2." alter="Wild shape"}

::ab[Snowcaster (Su)]{icon=power-magic l=9 ability="An arctic druid can see normally in ice storm, sleet storm, or similar natural snowstorms. In addition, she can prepare any druid spell with the fire subtype as a cold spell, with an identical effect but inflicting cold damage instead of fire damage." replace="Venom immunity"}

::ab[Flurry Form (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power-boost-magic ability="An arctic druid can assume the form of a swirling column of snow equivalent to ‹spell/gaseous form› at will. While in this form, she gains a circumstance bonus on Stealth checks made in cold terrain equal to her druid level." replace="A thousand faces"}

ashvawg_tamer.name: Ashvawg Tamer
description: ## Ashvawg Tamer
‹SOURCE Pathfinder #5: Sins of the Saviors/71›

The ashvawg tamer is a variant class that allows a druid to bond with strange and monstrous creatures. Given the unusual nature of these companions, the tamer also learns ways to strengthen his valuable ally. Most tamers worship ‹faith/Lamashtu›, the Demon Queen, though a few have discovered these practices independently. Tamers develop a stronger bond with their companion than some of the most powerful druids or rangers, and often travel to exotic locations to find strange specimens to tame, with bragging rights going to those with the most unusual creature.
::cskill{lose=diplo~heal gain=intm~ka}

::ab[Bestial Bond (Sp)]{l=4 icon=aid-lower ability="The druid can call out to savage powers of the wilds, entreating them to aid her and her companion. Doing so allows her to cast ‹spell/cure serious wounds›, ‹spell/neutralize poison›, or ‹spell/remove disease› on her animal companion as a spell-like ability by channeling the power of nature. The druid does not need to have these spells prepared as they come directly from the goddess. Using this ability, however, causes the druid to take 1d4 points of ‹rule/Wisdom damage›. Any effect that prevents this Wisdom damage from occurring also prevents the use of this ability." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Variant Companion (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost note="The druid adds additional non-animals to her alternate list of ‹ability/animal companion«s›. If she chooses one of these creatures as an animal companion, she may use her *wild empathy* ability to influence the creature (even though it is not an animal and its intelligence may be above 2), though she takes a -4 penalty on this check. If the druid and the companion share a common language these checks are not needed. If the companion's intelligence is 3 or higher, the number of &quot&tricks&quot& the druid can teach it refers to the number of commands the creature understands regardless of language." imp1="‹monster/Darkmantle›, ‹monster/krenshar›, ‹monster/shocker lizard›." imp4="(Level - 3) ‹monster/Hippogriff›" imp10="(Level - 9) ‹monster/Girallon›, ‹monster/griffon›, ‹monster/owlbear›, ‹monster/sea cat›" imp13="(Level - 12) ‹monster/Bulette›, ‹monster/chimera›"}

::ab[Undying Bond (Sp)]{l=6 icon=boost ability="The druid can use her *bestial bond* ability to cast ‹spell/resurrection› on her animal companion." replace="Daily use of *wild shape* at 6th level~venom immunity" special="Hereafter, the druid has one fewer daily use of the *wild shape* ability than a standard druid of her level."}

::ab[Exotic Companion (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost note="The druid adds additional non-animals to her alternate list of animal companions, all of them with higher intelligence and strange shapes. It otherwise functions as the *variant companion* ability." replace="Venom immunity" imp7="(Level - 6) ‹monster/Worg›" imp10="(Level - 9) ‹monster/Ettercap›, ‹monster/manticore›, ‹monster/minotaur›, ‹monster/winter wolf›" imp13="(Level - 12) ‹monster/Behir›, ‹monster/dragonne›, ‹monster/hieracosphinx›"}

>*@HL[Note:] This "alternate class" was created before the concept of archetypes was finalized by Paizo, and has been back-ported into archetype form. The original source lists the abilities being replaced, but does not specify what each is being replaced by.*

bat_shaman.name: Bat Shaman
description: ## Bat Shaman
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/42›

The bat shaman's totem is the agile bat, flitting and turning with incredible speed through even the most convoluted mazes. Her enemies do not know when she will appear, and when she does, she strikes fast and hard before disappearing into the night.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A bat shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a ‹companion/dire_>bat›. If choosing a domain, the bat shaman must choose from ‹domain/Air›, ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Darkness› (Night subdomain), and ‹domain/Trickery› (Deception subdomain)." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A bat shaman can use *wild empathy* with bats as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp5="The druid may select a new option: **Movement** (fly speed 30 ft. &#91&average&#93&)" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
A bat shaman may adopt an aspect of the bat while retaining her normal form. The druid may select from the following sets of bonuses and abilities:
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (bite [1d4 for a Medium shaman])
- **Senses** (‹umr/blindsense› 20 ft.)
While using *totem transformation,* the bat shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (bats only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="A bat shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon bats, and these summoned creatures gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to any bat to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates."}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost ability="A bat shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of a bat, she instead uses her druid level + 2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="A bat shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Acrobatic›, ‹feat/Agile Maneuvers›, ‹feat/Improved Initiative›, ‹feat/Lightning Reflexes›, or ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Perception)." imp13="The druid gains another bonus feat from the list above." imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

bear_shaman.name: Bear Shaman
description: ## Bear Shaman
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/102›
A shaman with this focus calls upon the mighty bear, titan of the woodlands and mountains, a paragon of strength and ferocity, and yet also a quiet protector rich in wisdom.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A bear shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a ‹companion/bear›. If choosing a domain, the bear shaman must choose from the ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Earth›, ‹domain/Protection›, and ‹domain/Strength› domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A bear shaman can use *wild empathy* with bears and wolverines as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
A bear shaman may adopt an aspect of the bear while retaining her normal form. She gains one of the following bonuses:
- **Movement** (+10 enhancement bonus to land speed, +4 racial bonus on Swim checks)
- **Senses** (low-light vision, ‹umr/scent›)
- **Toughness** (+2 natural armor bonus to AC, ‹feat/Endurance› feat)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (bite [1d6] and 2 claws [1d4] for a Medium shaman, +2 to CMB on grapple checks).
While using *totem transformation,* the bear shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (mammals only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="A bear shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon bears, and summoned bears gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to any bear to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates." replace="A thousand faces"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost ability="A bear shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of a bear, she instead uses her druid level + 2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="A bear shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Diehard›, Endurance, ‹feat/Great Fortitude›, ‹feat/Improved Great Fortitude›, ‹feat/Toughness›." imp13="The druid gains another feat from the list above."  imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

blight_druid.name: Blight Druid
description: ## Blight Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/98›

The devoted servants of nature corrupted, ruined, and destroyed, blight druids are the caretakers of lands ravaged by natural disaster. While some are devoted to reforming and reclaiming lands despoiled by the ravages of civilization, others seek out the more rapacious violence inherent in nature and feed the creeping rot and decay that brings an end to all things.

::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{icon=down-power choice="A blight druid may not bond with an animal companion, but may either call a ‹sidekick/familiar› as a ‹class/wizard› of her druid level or select from the ‹domain/Darkness›, ‹domain/Death›, and ‹domain/Destruction› domains in addition to those normally available." alter="Nature bond"}

::ab[Vermin Empathy (Su)]{icon=power ability="A blight druid can improve the attitude of vermin as a normal druid can with animals. Vermin have a starting attitude of unfriendly. The blight druid can also improve the attitude of animals and mindless undead creatures that were formerly animals, but she takes a -4 penalty on the check unless the animal or undead has a disease special attack." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Miasma (Ex)]{l=5 icon=lower passive="If a blight druid is adjacent to a creature at the beginning of its turn, the creature must succeed at a Fortitude save with a DC of 10 + ‹misc/half› the druid's level + the druid's Wisdom modifier or become ‹misc/sickened› for 1 round. A creature of the animal, fey, or plant type that fails its save is ‹misc/nauseated› for 1 round and sickened for 1 minute thereafter. If the creature makes its save, it is immune to this effect for 24 hours, as are creatures immune to disease." replace="Trackless step~resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Blightblooded (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="A blight druid gains immunity to all diseases, including natural and supernatural diseases. She also becomes immune to effects that would cause her to become sickened or nauseated." replace="Venom immunity"}

::ab[Plaguebearer (Su)]{icon=lower l=13 passive="Any creature that strikes a blight druid with a touch attack, unarmed strike, or natural weapon must succeed at a Fortitude save with a DC of 10 + 1/2 the druid's level + the druid's Wisdom modifier or contract a disease, as the ‹spell/contagion› spell. If the creature makes its save, it is immune to this effect for 24 hours." replace="A thousand faces"}

boar_shaman.name: Boar Shaman
description: ## Boar Shaman
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/43›
A boar shaman chooses the stolid and ferocious boar as her totem. Content to be left alone, she becomes one of the most dangerous creatures of the wild when provoked.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A boar shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a ‹companion/boar›. If choosing a domain, the boar shaman must choose from the ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Destruction› (Rage), ‹domain/Protection›, and ‹domain/Strength› domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A boar shaman can use *wild empathy* with porcine creatures as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
A boar shaman may adopt an aspect of the boar while retaining her normal form. The druid may select from the following sets of bonuses and abilities:
- **Movement** (+10 enhancement bonus to land speed)
- **Senses** (low-light vision, ‹umr/scent›)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (gore [1d8 for a Medium druid], +2 on combat maneuver checks to overrun)
- **Toughness** (+2 natural armor bonus to AC, ‹feat/Endurance› feat).
While using *totem transformation,* the boar shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (boars and related animals only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="A boar shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon ‹monster/boar«s› or other porcine creatures, and these summoned creatures gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to any porcine summon to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates."}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost passive="A boar shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of a boar, she instead uses her druid level +2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="A boar shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Bleeding Critical›, ‹feat/Blind-Fight›, ‹feat/Diehard›, or ‹feat/Improved Overrun›." imp13="The druid gains another feat from the list above."  imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

cave_druid.name: Cave Druid
description: ## Cave Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/99›

Far from the green fields of the world above lies a lightless expanse beneath the surface. This darkling fairyland is not without beauty and natural wonders of its own, and a few druids seek to preserve this hidden realm and purge it of the fell horrors that creep up from below.

::ab[Cavesense (Ex)]{icon=skill-boost passive="A cave druid adds ‹skill/Knowledge (dungeoneering)› (INT) rather than Knowledge (geography) as a class skill and gains a +2 bonus on Knowledge (dungeoneering) and Survival skill checks." replace="Nature sense"}

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=boost-down choice="A cave druid may select the ‹domain/Darkness› domain in addition to the choices normally allowed, but may not select the Air or Weather domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A cave druid can influence oozes, rather than magical beasts, with a -4 penalty on her *wild empathy* check." alter="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Tunnelrunner (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A cave druid can move through areas of rubble or narrow passages that require ‹misc/squeezing› at her normal movement rate and without penalty." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Lightfoot (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A cave druid cannot be detected with ‹umr/tremorsense›." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Resist Subterranean Corruption (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A cave druid gains a +2 bonus on saves against exceptional, supernatural, and spell-like abilities of oozes and aberrations." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{icon=down-boost l=6 ability="A cave druid gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for this ability is equal to her druid level - 2. She cannot use *wild shape* to adopt a plant form." alter="Wild shape" imp10="The cave druid can assume the form of a Small or Medium ooze as if using ‹spell/beast shape III›." imp12="The druid can assume the form of a Tiny or Large ooze as if using ‹spell/beast shape IV› (treating the ooze as if it were a magical beast without a natural armor bonus)." special="When in ooze form, the cave druid has no discernible anatomy and is immune to poison, sneak attacks, and critical hits."}

death_druid.name: Death Druid
description: ## Death Druid
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/50›
Some druids believe that birth and death are the most important parts of the natural cycle. They focus on destroying undead and putting troubled souls to rest.

::ab[Phantom]{icon=power passive="A death druid attracts phantoms like a ‹class/spiritualist›, though she focuses her efforts on putting those phantoms to rest. A death druid is continually attempting to figure out how to send her ‹sidekick/phantom› to the afterlife (usually by completing the phantom's unfinished business as determined by the GM). If she is able to do so, she gains a new phantom with no additional effort on her part, sometime during the next week." imp1="She gains the spiritualist's *phantom* and *etheric tether* class features." imp4="She gains the *bonded manifestation* class feature." imp14="She gains the *spiritual bond* class feature." special="She does not gain any of the other spiritualist abilities related to phantoms. The death druid treats her druid level as her spiritualist level for these abilities." replace="Nature bond~wild shape"}

::ab[Soul Magic]{icon=learn note="A death druid adds the following spells to her druid spell list at the given spell levels." s0="Grave words" s1="Sanctify corpse" s2="Calm spirit~~false life~~ghostbane dirge~~spiritual weapon" s3="Speak with dead" s4="Speak with haunt~~spiritual ally" s5="Breath of life~~call spirit~~mass ghostbane dirge" s6="Circle of death~~undeath to death" replace="Nature sense~wild empathy"}
::ab[Resist Death's Call (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A death druid gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against death effects, negative energy, and energy drain." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Negative Immunity (Su)]{l=9 icon=def passive="A death druid is immune to negative energy and energy drain." replace="Venom immunity"}

defender_of_the_true_world.name: Defender of the True World
description: ## Defender of the True World
‹SOURCE Monster Hunter's Handbook/10›
Some druids specialize in protecting Golarion from the threat of First World inhabitants. They are devoted to tracking down, diverting, and slaying fey threats.
::cskill{gain=diplo}
::ab[Bonus Languages]{icon=power note="A defender of the true world can select Aklo, Sylvan, or Undercommon as bonus languages, in addition to the normal list of bonus languages available."}
::ab[Enemy of the First World]{icon=down note="A defender of the true world cannot use ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon fey creatures." alter="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Fey Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A defender of the true world gains a competence bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Intimidate checks when interacting with fey creatures. This bonus is equal to 2 + ‹misc/one-fourth› of her druid level." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Fey Stalker (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="The defender of the true world's animal companion and any creatures she summons with *summon nature's ally* gain a +1 morale bonus on attack and damage rolls against fey creatures." increment="The bonus~3~5" replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Feybane (Ex)]{l=4 icon=down-boost passive="A defender of the true world doesn't gain a bonus on saving throws against spells and effects that utilize or target plants. She instead treats her natural attacks as cold iron for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction, and she gains a +2 bonus on caster level checks to overcome the spell resistance of fey creatures." alter="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Beguiling Immunity (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="A defender of the true world becomes immune to the mind-affecting effects of fey." replace="Venom immunity"}

::ab[First World Deceiver (Su)]{l=13 icon=magic ability="A defender of the true world gains the ability to change her appearance as per ‹spell/alter self›, except she can take the form of a humanoid or fey creature. Her base land speed from this ability cannot exceed 60 feet." replace="A thousand faces"}

desert_druid.name: Desert Druid
description: ## Desert Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/99›

Not all climates are verdant paradises, but even in the sere deserts there is life - though often hidden from the sun and rarely friendly - and raw, desolate beauty. Here desert druids come to pay homage, protect and maintain the few habitable locales, and witness nature's majesty in all its burning, merciless glory.

::ab[Desert Native (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-power passive="A desert druid gains a bonus on Initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level in desert terrain, and she cannot be tracked in such environments." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Sandwalker (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A desert druid suffers no penalty to speed or on Acrobatics or Stealth checks when moving through sandy or desert terrain." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Desert Endurance (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def-boost passive="A desert druid ignores the effects of a hot climate as if using ‹spell/endure elements›. She also has a reduced need to eat and drink, as if wearing a ‹magic-ring/ring of sustenance› (though normal sleep is still required)." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{icon=down l=6 ability="A desert druid gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for this ability is equal to her druid level - 2. A desert druid cannot use *wild shape* to adopt a plant form." alter="Wild shape" imp10="She can assume the form of a Small or Medium vermin." imp12="She can take the form of a Tiny or Large vermin." imp14="She can assume the form of a Diminutive or Huge vermin." special="Starting at 10th level, this effect functions as ‹spell/beast shape IV› (treating the vermin as an animal to determine its ability and natural armor modifiers)."}

::ab[Shaded Vision (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="A desert druid becomes immune to blinding and dazzling effects and gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against ‹umr/gaze› attacks and illusions of the figment and pattern subschools." replace="Venom immunity"}

::ab[Dunemeld (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power-boost-magic ability="A desert druid can assume the form of a swirling mass of sand at will. This is equivalent to ‹spell/gaseous form›, but the druid gains a land and burrow speed of 10 feet rather than a fly speed. While in this form, the druid gains a circumstance bonus on Stealth checks made in desert terrain equal to her druid level." replace="A thousand faces"}

devolutionist.name: Devolutionist
description: ## Devolutionist
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/50›

Full of simmering hatred toward civilization and those who dwell within it, devolutionists believe that the world would be better off without any sentient life at all. They transform intelligent creatures and domesticated animals into throwbacks to their feral ancestors.

::ab[Devolved Companion (Ex)]{icon=info choice="A devolutionist must choose a devolved humanoid as an animal companion for her *nature bond.* Use the stats for an ‹companion/ape› animal companion." imp4="The devolved humanoid doesn't increase to size Large (it still gains all the other benefits at 4th level)." alter="Nature bond"}

::ab[Undomesticate (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="A devolutionist can spend 1 minute with a willing or restrained domesticated animal to undo the effects of domestication, transforming it into a wild animal and causing it to lose any tricks it knew. The animal no longer recognizes any humanoids as friends or allies and gladly tears former playmates apart for food. Handle Animal checks don't work on an undomesticated creature, though *wild empathy* does." special="This ability doesn't function on animal companions, special mounts, or the like, and other exceptional domesticated animals can attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the devolutionist's druid level + her Wisdom modifier) to negate the effect and become immune to it for 24 hours. Though this ability is instantaneous, ‹spell/break enchantment›, ‹spell/limited wish›, ‹spell/miracle›, or ‹spell/wish› can reverse the effects." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
:::ab{l=9 icon=magic action=Ability title="Devolution (Sp)" replace="Venom immunity" special="This ability counters and dispels ‹spell/awaken›, and only *awaken, miracle,* or *wish* can reverse it."}

A devolutionist can perform a 24-hour ritual to irrevocably transform a humanoid or animal into a devolved feral state. There is no saving throw, but the target must be present and stay relatively still for the entire ritual, which typically requires a willing or restrained target. An affected humanoid's Intelligence score is instantly reduced to 2; its type changes to ‹type/animal›; it loses all spellcasting and abilities that require intelligence; it can't wield manufactured weapons; it gains two ‹umr/natural>claw» attacks› and a bite attack (all dealing 1d4 points of damage for a Medium humanoid or 1d3 for Small humanoids); and it can't use skills other than Acrobatics, Climb, Escape Artist, Fly, Intimidate, Perception, Stealth, Survival, and Swim.

An affected animal is *undomesticated* (see above) if applicable and then either gains the ‹template/advanced› template, except without any increases to mental ability scores, or becomes a dire version of the animal (if a dire version exists and at the GM's discretion). The devolved creature starts out friendly toward the devolutionist, though it typically tries to kill and devour other sentient humanoids. The devolutionist has no special empathy or connection with a creature she devolves, and if it was her animal companion before, it ceases to be so. The druid can communicate her desires to a devolved creature using *wild empathy.*

dinosaur_druid.name: Dinosaur Druid
description: ## Dinosaur Druid
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/47›
A dinosaur druid speaks for the spirit of prehistoric nature, even taking the form of great beasts of legend.
;;;ab{title="Summon Dinosaur (Sp)" action=Ability icon=magic alter="Spontaneous casting"}

A dinosaur druid can summon a wide range of dinosaurs and related prehistoric beasts using ‹spell/summon nature's ally› spells. She adds the following options to these spells when using *spontaneous casting* to cast such a spell.
:::div{className=hanging}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally I]{all=Compsognathus link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally II]{all=Dimorphodon~troodon link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally III]{all=Plesiosaurus~velociraptor link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally IV]{all=Dimetrodon~pachycephalosaurus~parasaurolophus link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally V]{all=Amargasaurus~ceratosaurus~iguanodon~kentrosaurus~styracosaurus link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally VI]{all=Allosaurus~quetzalcoatlus~tylosaurus link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally VII]{all=Therizinosaurus link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally VIII]{all=Diplodocus~spinosaurus link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally IX]{all=Giganotosaurus link=monster hl em}
::ab[Dinosaur Bond (Ex)]{icon=info choice="A dinosaur druid must choose an animal companion as her *nature's bond,* and the animal companion must be a ‹family/dinosaur›." alter="Nature's bond"}

::ab[Dinosaur Shape (Su)]{icon=boost-down flavor="A dinosaur druid specializes in assuming the form of dinosaurs when using *wild shape.*" ability="When she assumes the form of a dinosaur via *wild shape,* she gains a +2 size bonus to her Constitution in addition to the other modifiers granted by *beast shape.* When she uses *wild shape* to assume the form of a creature other than a dinosaur, the *wild shape* effect lasts for only 1 minute per level, rather than 1 hour per level." alter="Wild shape"}
::ab[Primeval Voice (Su)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A dinosaur druid gains the ability to speak with dinosaurs (as per ‹spell/speak with animals›) at all times." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

draconic_druid.name: Draconic Druid
description: ## Draconic Druid
‹SOURCE Legacy of Dragons/24›
Some druids believe that dragons are the ultimate expression of nature, combining elemental fury with majestic beauty. These druids consort with dragons and eventually transform into draconic forms.

::ab[Drake Companion]{icon=power ability="A draconic druid gains a ‹sidekick/drake« companion› instead of an animal companion." replace="Nature bond~wild empathy~woodland stride~venom immunity~a thousand faces~timeless body"}
::ab[Dragon Sense]{icon=boost flavor="A draconic druid studies dragons and their history." passive="She gains a +2 bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Knowledge (history) checks." replace="Nature sense"}

::ab[Resist Dragon's Might]{icon=def passive="A draconic druid gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against the spells, spell-like abilities, and supernatural abilities of dragons." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Dragon Shape]{icon=down-boost l=4 ability="A draconic druid can't use *wild shape* to change into any of the usual forms available to a druid. Instead, she can use *wild shape* to change into a dragon-scaled version of herself with long fangs, gaining a +1 natural armor bonus to her AC and a ‹umr/natural>bite» attack› appropriate for her size (1d6 points of damage for a Medium druid) but otherwise retaining her usual form." imp10="She can spend two uses of *wild shape* to transform into a Medium dragon as per ‹spell/form of the dragon I›." imp12="She can spend two uses to change into a Large dragon as per ‹spell/form of the dragon II›." special="Each time that a draconic druid uses *wild shape* counts as a separate casting of the *form of the dragon* spell for the purpose of refreshing her uses of her breath weapon."}

dragon_shaman.name: Dragon Shaman
description: ## Dragon Shaman
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/37›

Your totem is the legendary dragon, fearsome and deadly yet cunning and wise, a creature born of pure magic and raw elemental fury, bound within a shell of fangs, claws, and scales that few dare to challenge. Though your initial focus is on dragons' mundane cousins, as your powers grow you become attuned to actual dragons.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A dragon shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a ‹companion/crocodile› or ‹companion/monitor lizard›. If choosing a domain, the dragon shaman must choose from the ‹domain/Air›, ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Destruction›, ‹domain/Earth›, ‹domain/Fire›, ‹domain/War›, and ‹domain/Water› domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A dragon shaman can use her *wild empathy* ability with lizards as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp5="The druid may select a new option: **Movement** (fly speed 30 ft. &#91&average&#93&)" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
A dragon shaman may adopt an aspect of the dragon while retaining her normal form. She gains one of the following bonuses:
- **Senses** (low-light vision, +4 racial bonus to Perception)
- **Toughness** (+2 natural armor bonus to AC, ‹feat/Endurance› feat)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (bite [1d6] and 2 claws [1d4] for a Medium shaman, +2 bonus on CMB on grapple checks).
While using *totem transformation,* the dragon shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (lizards only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="A dragon shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon lizards, and summoned lizards gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› to any lizard to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates." replace="A thousand faces"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down passive="A dragon shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 4. If she takes on the form of a lizard, she instead uses her unmodified druid level." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Dragon Bite (Su)]{l=8 icon=melee passive="A dragon shaman's bite attack (whether using *totem transformation* or *wild shape* to take the form of a lizard) deals +1d6 points of energy damage (acid, cold, electricity, or fire). She chooses what kind of energy damage that she deals each time she bites." replace="The additional use of *wild shape* at 8th level"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="A dragon shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Combat Casting›, ‹feat/Dazzling Display›, ‹feat/Magical Aptitude›, ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Knowledge &#91&arcana&#93&), and ‹feat/Spell Penetration›." imp13="The druid gains another feat from the list above."  imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

drovier.name: Drovier
description: ## Drovier
‹SOURCE Magic Tactics Toolbox/6›

Droviers view their allies and adventuring companions as a herd or pack to be protected and directed, with variations on that theme ranging from culture to culture. The orcs of Belkzen see droviers as alpha predators, offering tribal warriors totemic power in exchange for obedience in combat. The elves of the Mwangi Expanse regard them as spokespeople for the jungle, adopting aspects that help preserve and defend the land.
::ab[Nature's Bond (Ex)]{icon=info note="A drovier must take the ‹domain/Animal› domain with this ability, or the ‹druiddomain/Eagle› or ‹druiddomain/Wolf› animal domains." alter="Nature's bond"}

::ab[Communal Aspect (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic flavor="A drovier can imbue her allies (including herself) with an aspect of a wild animal." standard="She chooses an animal totem and grants the abilities of that totem to every ally within 30 feet. This otherwise functions like the spell ‹spell/animal aspect›. If she uses the ability to grant a new aspect, any previous aspect immediately ends." useL=druid~10 useNC=10 useUnit=minute imp6="The drovier's communal aspect ability grants the benefits of the spell ‹spell/greater animal aspect›. The drovier can still choose only one aspect to grant her allies." imp8="The drovier can choose to grant her allies the benefits of the spell ‹spell/aspect of the falcon› instead of an animal aspect." imp10="The drovier can choose to grant her allies the benefits of the spell ‹spell/aspect of the bear› instead of an animal aspect." imp12="The drovier can choose to grant her allies the benefits of the spell ‹spell/aspect of the stag› instead of an animal aspect." imp14="The drovier can choose to grant her allies the benefits of the spell ‹spell/aspect of the wolf› rather than an animal aspect." imp20="The drovier can use this ability at will." replace="Wild shape"}

eagle_shaman.name: Eagle Shaman
description: ## Eagle Shaman
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/102›
A shaman with this totem calls upon the noble eagle, stern and proud, soaring high above the world with keen and pitiless eyes that miss nothing.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="An eagle shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a ‹companion/bird« (eagle)›. If choosing a domain, the eagle shaman must choose from the ‹domain/Air›, ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Nobility›, and ‹domain/Weather› domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="An eagle shaman can use *wild empathy* with birds as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp5="The druid may select a new option: **Movement** (fly speed 30 ft. &#91&average&#93&)" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
An eagle shaman may adopt an aspect of the eagle while retaining her normal form. The druid may select from the following bonuses:
- **Senses** (low-light vision, +4 racial bonus to Perception)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (bite [1d4], 2 talons [1d4] for a Medium shaman)
While using *totem transformation,* the eagle shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (birds only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="An eagle shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon ‹monster/eagle«s›, ‹monster/roc«s›, and ‹monster/giant eagle«s› (added to the 4th-level list), and these summoned creatures gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to any eagle or roc to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates."}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost passive="An eagle shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of an eagle or roc, she instead uses her druid level + 2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="An eagle shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Flyby Attack›, ‹feat/Improved Lightning Reflexes›, ‹feat/Lightning Reflexes›, ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Perception), or ‹feat/Wind Stance›." imp13="The druid gains another feat from the list above."  imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

elemental_ally.name: Elemental Ally
description: ## Elemental Ally
‹SOURCE Monster Summoner's Handbook/16›

An elemental ally is a druid who has crafted powerful bonds of trust and friendship with one ally from each of the four primary elemental planes. Elemental allies are most common in Qadira, where they are considered similar to genie-binders.
:::ab{l=1 icon=power title="Elemental Eidolons" action=Ability replace="Nature's bond~wild shape"}

An elemental ally has four elemental eidolons she can summon, one each for the elements of air, earth, fire, and water. Their abilities and statistics are determined using the rules for ‹eidolon/elemental› eidolons for the ‹class/unchained summoner› class, as if the elemental ally were a summoner of her druid level, except they gain no additional evolution pool (just those evolutions from their base form and base evolutions from their subtype).

Each of the four elementals has its own base form, skills, and feats. The elemental ally can summon one of these elementals with the same 1-minute ritual a summoner normally uses to do so. The elemental ally can't summon an eidolon or cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› if she currently has an eidolon summoned. She also can't summon an eidolon if she currently has a creature summoned through other means. If one of the elemental ally's eidolons is killed, she cannot summon any eidolons for 24 hours. The elemental ally can dismiss her eidolon as a @HL[standard action].

As the elemental ally gains levels, her elemental eidolons' base statistics and base evolutions increase as if her druid level were her summoner level. The eidolons gain the *darkvision, link, share spells, evasion, ability score increase, devotion, multiattack,* and *improved evasion* abilities at the appropriate levels, but never gain an evolution pool. Abilities and spells that grant additional evolution points to eidolons do not function for elemental eidolons, though any ability that would grant evolution points to an animal companion does work. The elemental ally does not gain *life link* or any other class features a summoner gains in relation to her eidolon.
:::ab{icon=power title="Elemental Empathy (Ex)" action=Ability replace="Wild empathy"}

An elemental ally can improve the attitude of a creature of the elemental subtype. This ability functions just like a Diplomacy check to improve the attitude of a person. The elemental ally rolls 1d20 and adds her druid level and her Charisma modifier to determine the *elemental empathy* check result.

To use *elemental empathy,* the elemental ally and the elemental must be able to study each other, which means that they must be within 30 feet of one another under normal conditions. Generally, influencing an elemental in this way takes 1 minute, but as with influencing people, it might take more or less time.
An elemental ally can also use this ability to influence a non-elemental outsider with the air, earth, fire, or water subtype, but she takes a -4 penalty on the check.

::ab[Elemental Magic]{icon=boost passive="The elemental ally can target elementals (including her elemental eidolons) with any spell she casts that targets animals, even though elementals can't normally be targeted by such spells."}
::ab[Elemental Resistance (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="An elemental ally gains ‹umr/resist› acid, cold, electricity, and fire 5." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

feral_child.name: Feral Child
description: ## Feral Child
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/74›

Some youths, abandoned in the wilderness and then raised by animals, are so connected with their adoptive home and family that they become feral. Suspicious of civilized society, these foundlings often choose allegiance to the wild over their human forebears. A feral child has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Human}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A feral child loses proficiency with the scimitar, scythe, and sickle and with shields."}
::cskill{gain=acro lose=fly~prof}

::ab[Illiteracy]{l=1 icon=down note="A feral child is unable to read and write, though she may learn by taking 1 rank of Linguistics. She does not gain Druidic as a free language and cannot select Sylvan as a bonus language."}
::ab[Improved Unarmed Strike]{l=1 icon=power ability="A feral child gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Beast Family (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost choice="A feral child may choose one specific type of animal as the type that raised her. She gains a +2 circumstance bonus on Handle Animal and *wild empathy* checks with animals of that type, and she can communicate with them as if using a continual ‹spell/speak with animals› spell-like ability, but this ability is nonmagical."}
::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power choice="A feral child must select an animal companion as her nature bond."}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A feral child gains the ‹ability/favored terrain› ability as a ‹class/ranger› of her class level. A feral child may not choose urban as a favored terrain." replace="Trackless step~a thousand faces"}

::ab[Native Cunning (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A feral child gains ‹ability/trap sense› as a ‹class/barbarian› of equal level, and in her favored terrain, she immediately receives a Perception check to notice traps within 10 feet, as the trap spotter rogue talent." order=ac choice="She may choose one combat maneuver, and gains a bonus equal to her trap sense bonus to her CMD against that maneuver." imp6="She may choose another combat maneuver to gain the same bonus in." imp9="She may gain a third such bonus." imp12="She may select a fourth combat maneuver for a bonus." imp15="She may select a fifth such maneuver." imp18="She may select a sixth such maneuver." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Native Fortitude (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A feral child gains a +1 bonus on saving throws against disease, exhaustion, fatigue, fear, and poison. When she is in her favored terrain, she instead applies her favored terrain bonus on such saving throws. She also recovers from ‹rule/ability damage›, ‹misc/exhaust«ion<ed›, and ‹misc/fatigue<d› at twice the normal rate." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Native Call (Su)]{l=9 icon=boost passive="When in her favored terrain, for any ‹spell/summon nature's ally› spells a feral child uses to summon animals that are native to that terrain, she treats the duration of the spell as if she were 2 levels higher." imp17="When the feral child uses *summon nature's ally* spells to summon such animals, those animals gain a +2 bonus to both their Strength and Constitution ability scores. This stacks with the effects of the ‹feat/Augment Summoning› feat." replace="Venom immunity~timeless body"}

feral_shifter.name: Feral Shifter
description: ## Feral Shifter
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/92›

A feral shifter internalizes her communion with and mastery over animals. Instead of forming a bond with an animal companion or an aspect of nature, she alters her own essence or being as homage to the noble creatures of the wild. More in tune with transformation and animal bodies than a normal druid, a feral shifter blurs the line between humanoid and beast.

::ab[Animal Focus (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic swift="A feral shifter can take on the aspect of an animal, gaining a bonus or special ability based on the type of animal emulated. This functions as the hunter's ‹ability/animal focus› class feature." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="She can emulate only one animal at a time." replace="Nature bond"}

::ab[Second Animal Focus (Su)]{l=9 icon=boost passive="When a feral shifter uses her animal focus ability, she selects two different animal aspects for herself instead of one." replace="Venom immunity~a thousand faces~timeless body"}

feyspeaker.name: Feyspeaker
description: ## Feyspeaker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/26›

Some druids hear the whispers of the natural world like fey creatures do, and learn to mimic those tantalizing murmurs in order to influence the minds of both wild beasts and civilized people. These feyspeakers are often touched by the fey in some way, such as mortals who dared to eat the food and drink the wine while in the hidden halls of the fey.

::ab[Fey Magic (Part One)]{l=1 icon=down-learn flavor="A feyspeaker allows the strange idylls of the fey to guide her magic." note="The feyspeaker uses her Charisma score instead of her Wisdom score as her key spellcasting ability score (to determine her spell DCs, ‹rule/bonus spells› per day, concentration checks, and so on). A feyspeaker loses the ability to spontaneously cast *summon nature's ally* spells." imp4="A feyspeaker adds one enchantment or illusion spell from the ‹main/spells_>sorcerer›/‹main/spells_>wizard› spell list to her druid spell list as a spell of 1 level higher (for instance, at 4th level, she could add ‹spell/color spray› as a 2nd-level druid spell). In order to select a spell, she must be of a level high enough (including the increased spell level) to cast it. Once selected, the spell cannot be changed." imp6="She adds another bonus spell, following the same rules and restrictions above." imp8="She adds a third such bonus spell." imp10="She gains a fourth bonus spell." imp12="She gains a fifth bonus spell." imp14="She gains a sixth bonus spell." imp16="She gains a seventh bonus spell." imp18="She gains an eighth bonus spell." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Fey Speech (Ex)]{l=1 icon=skill-power-boost-down flavor="A feyspeaker's words carry a tiny fragment of the otherworldly power of the fey's voices." passive="She gains ‹skill/Bluff› (CHA), ‹skill/Diplomacy› (CHA), ‹skill/Disguise› (CHA), and ‹skill/Sense Motive› (WIS) as class skills. A feyspeaker also learns Sylvan as a bonus language. In addition, the feyspeaker gains a number of skill ranks at each level equal to 6 + her Intelligence modifier, instead of 4 + her Intelligence modifier." order=pn note="The feyspeaker's base attack bonus from her druid levels is equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level (much like a sorcerer's base attack bonus is calculated), rather than the usual druid base attack bonus." replace="Nature sense" alter="Base attack bonus~class skills~skills ranks per level"}

::ab[Wild Mischief (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A feyspeaker can fool an animal into thinking there is food, a potential mate, or a predator nearby. This ability functions just like a Bluff check to deceive a character. To determine the result of the *wild mischief* check, the feyspeaker rolls 1d20 and adds her druid level and her Charisma modifier. As with Bluff checks, *wild mischief* checks are modified depending on the believability of the false information. A typical domestic animal is not particularly suspicious of the feyspeaker (no circumstance modifier), whereas wild animals are usually suspicious (-5 penalty on the check). To use *wild mischief,* the feyspeaker and the animal must be able to study each other, which means they must be within 30 feet of one another under normal conditions. Generally, influencing an animal in this way takes 1 minute, but as when influencing people, it could take more or less time." special="A feyspeaker can also use this ability to influence a magical beast with an Intelligence score of 1 or 2, but she takes a -4 penalty on the check." replace="The druid's proficiency with medium armor"}

::ab[Fey Magic (Part Two)]{l=6 icon=magic-down flavor="A feyspeaker allows the strange idylls of the fey to guide her magic." note="The feyspeaker gains the *wild shape* ability and gains all benefits related to *wild shape* 2 druid levels later than usual. She can never use *wild shape* to transform into an elemental." alter="Wild shape"}

fungal_pilgrim.name: Fungal Pilgrim
description: ## Fungal Pilgrim
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Darklands/16›

Druids often work to preserve the natural world, but some seek to further emulate it. Fungal pilgrims are druids seeking transformation into a more plant-like existence. These unique adherents of nature's creed see fungi as the means to such ends, as many fungi - particularly those of the Darklands' naturally work their way into the bodies of living creatures.
::ab[Devotion to Ascension (Ex)]{icon=info choice="A fungal pilgrim must select the ‹domain/Plant› domain for her nature bond if she does not select an animal companion." alter="Nature bond"}

:::ab{l=4 icon=magic action=Ability title="Spore Spawning" note="The fungal pilgrim @HLonly gets this ability if they selected the Plant domain as their *nature bond.*" replace="Wild shape" useInc=druid~2~4 order=nCu imp20="The druid can use this ability any number of times a day."}

A fungal pilgrim who selected the Plant domain for her nature bond gains the ability to grow fungal creatures from the spores of her body. Once per day, when casting a *summon nature's ally* spell to summon a single creature, the fungal pilgrim can infuse her fungal connection into the casting. The creature summoned must be selected from the ‹spell/summon nature's ally› list for 1 spell level lower than the spell cast. For example, if the fungal pilgrim casts *summon nature's ally III,* she must choose a creature from the list in *summon nature's ally II.*

Creatures summoned in this manner gain the ‹template/fungal creature› template, except they do not gain the *create spawn* and *poison spore cloud* special attacks. A creature summoned in this manner cannot be harvested for its poison.

::ab[Fungal Companion]{l=4 note="The fungal pilgrim @HLonly gets this ability if they selected an animal companion as their *nature bond.*" order=na icon=boost ability="A fungal pilgrim who selected an animal companion for her nature bond initiates a transformation in her companion. The animal companion gains the *fungal creature* template, but does not gain the *create spawn, poison spore cloud,* and *poisonous blood* special attacks." imp10="The animal companion gains the *poisonous blood* special attack." imp14="The animal companion gains the *poison spore* cloud special attack." imp20="The animal companion gains the *create spawn* special attack; created spawn are under the control of the fungal pilgrim. Spawn created in this way last for only 48 hours before rotting away." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Mycological Transformation (Ex)]{l=15 flavor="Fungal spores become fully integrated into the druid's body, inside and out." icon=def-down-magic passive="She gains all immunities granted by the ‹type/plant› creature type as well as the ‹umr/light sensitivity› universal monster rule. She counts as a plant for the purposes of effects that would negatively affect or damage plants. In addition, she no longer needs to eat or sleep in the typical manner, and instead gains the fungal creature's *rejuvenation* ability. She can perform her daily meditation during her rejuvenation." replace="Timeless body"}

goliath_druid.name: Goliath Druid
description: ## Goliath Druid
‹SOURCE Giant Hunter's Handbook/20›

Goliath druids hone their spiritual and magical connections to nature's largest creatures, especially dinosaurs, giants, and megafauna, revering these massive creatures as living relics of a primeval time when all creatures were more in harmony with nature. Despite this respect, a goliath druid doesn't hesitate to infiltrate giant groups who defile nature and destroy them from within.
Goliath druids have the following class features.
::cskill{gain=bluff~diplo~kl lose=craft~prof~ride}
::ab[Bonus Languages]{icon=info note="A goliath druid with an Intelligence bonus can select Giant as a bonus language."}

::ab[Primal Size]{icon=learn-magic ability="A goliath druid adds ‹spell/enlarge person› to her list of class spells. She can cast this spell only on herself. She can also channel stored spell energy to cast this spell without preparing it - she can lose a prepared spell of 1st level or higher to cast *enlarge person.*" replace="Nature sense"}

::ab[Primal Bond (Ex)]{icon=info choice="When a goliath druid forms a *nature bond,* if she selects a cleric domain, she must selected from the ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Destruction›, or ‹domain/Strength› domains, or the ‹domain/Ferocity›, ‹domain/Growth›, or ‹domain/Rage› subdomains. If the goliath druid selects an animal companion, she must select a ‹family/dinosaur› or ‹family/megafauna›. If she has a dinosaur or megafauna animal companion, she can target it with *enlarge person* even though the companion isn't of the humanoid type." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Primal Empathy (Ex)]{icon=down ability="A goliath druid's *wild empathy* functions only with creatures that are Large or larger." alter="Wild empathy"}
::ab[Face Nature's Might (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A goliath druid gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of giants." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=4 icon=down-boost note="The ability to *wild shape* into a creature of the animal type allows a goliath druid to assume only the form of a dinosaur or megafauna. She doesn't gain the ability to become an elemental or plant." imp6="The goliath druid can use *wild shape* to become a Large humanoid of the giant subtype. This functions as the ‹spell/alter self› spell, except the goliath druid gains a +4 size bonus to Strength, a -2 penalty to Dexterity, and a +1 natural armor bonus. If the Large humanoid form she takes has rock throwing, she gains ‹umr/rock throwing› (range 40 feet, 1d8 damage). If the form has the aquatic subtype, she gains the ‹subtype/aquatic› subtype and the ‹umr/amphibious› quality" imp12="When taking the form of a giant, the goliath druid's *wild shape* functions as ‹spell/giant form I›." imp14="The goliath druid can also use *wild shape* to change into a Huge giant. When taking the form of a giant, the goliath druid's *wild shape* functions as ‹spell/giant form II›." alter="Wild shape"}
;;;ab{title="Primal Summons" icon=boost action=Ability replace="Venom immunity~a thousand faces"}
A goliath druid adds the following creatures to the list of creatures she can normally summon using *summon nature's ally* spells.
:::div{className=hanging}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally I]{all=Compsognathus link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally II]{all=Dimorphodon link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally III]{all=Velociraptor link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally IV]{all=Ogre~parasaurolophus link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally V]{all=Glyptodon~iguanodon link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally VI]{all=Baluchitherium~megalania link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally VII]{all="Cliff giant" link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally VIII]{all=Spinosaurus link=monster hl em}
::list[Summon Nature's Ally IX]{all=Diplodocus link=monster hl em}

green_faith_initiate.name: Green Faith Initiate
description: ## Green Faith Initiate
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/141;Faiths and Philosophies/10›
Druids who are initiated into the magic of nature as strict students of the ‹faith/Green Faith«'s› traditions sometimes demonstrate abilities unique to their organization.

::ab[Mediator's Ear (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A Green Faith initiate is respected for her neutrality and even-handed attitude toward all people as well as other living things. She quickly develops a sense of other peoples' attitudes, much as she does with animals. A Green Faith initiate gains a bonus on Diplomacy and Sense Motive checks equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Zephyr Message (Sp)]{l=6 icon=magic ability="Once per day, a Green Faith initiate can cast ‹spell/whispering wind› as a spell-like ability, treating her druid level as her caster level." replace="Daily use of *wild shape* gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Path to Refuge (Sp)]{l=9 icon=magic ability="Once per day, a Green Faith initiate can travel as if with ‹spell/transport via plants› to any major Green Faith druid circle or important holy site. Each of these sites is marked with special standing stones that allow all Green Faith followers within range using *transport via plants* or ‹spell/tree stride› to sense their distance from it and the direction to it." imp14="She can use this ability twice a day." imp19="She can use this ability three times a day." replace="Venom immunity"}

::ab[Secrets across Lifetimes (Sp)]{l=10 icon=magic ability="A Green Faith initiate can use ‹spell/legend lore› once per week as a spell-like ability, drawing upon the knowledge of Green Faith archdruids recorded in secret across the generations in places where magic such as this can retrieve it. This ability does not require material components." replace="Daily use of *wild shape* gained at 10th level"}

::ab[A Thousand Voices (Su)]{l=13 icon=power ability="A Green Faith initiate can communicate with any creature that has a language or with any stone, as if using ‹spell/tongues› or ‹spell/stone tell›, respectively." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 replace="A thousand faces"}

green_scourge.name: Green Scourge
description: ## Green Scourge
‹SOURCE Monster Hunter's Handbook/7›

Green scourges are militant druids sworn to restore natural order. They're less concerned with nurturing the natural world than most druids, instead focusing their efforts on slaying corrupted creatures.

::ab[Nature's Armaments]{icon=magic order=an ability="A green scourge can lose a prepared 1st- or 2nd-level spell in order to cast ‹spell/shillelagh› or ‹spell/flame blade›, respectively. She can instead choose to lose a higher-level spell in order to increase the enhancement bonus of her *shillelagh,* or to add any of the following weapon special abilities to her *shillelagh* or *flame blade:* ‹magic-enh/bane› (aberrations only), ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/mighty cleaving›, ‹magic-enh/returning›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, ‹magic-enh/throwing›, or ‹magic-enh/vicious›." note="When adding weapon special abilities or increasing the enhancement bonus, the final level of the spell expended is 1 higher than the base spell level (2nd for *shillelagh* and 3rd for *flame blade*), increased by the additional special abilities' total base price modifier. For example, creating an *aberration-bane vicious flame blade* requires spending a 5th-level spell slot (3 for *flame blade,* +1 for *bane,* +1 for *vicious* = 5), which also counts as a 5th-level spell for purposes of dispelling." special="If the target weapon is a ‹eq-weapon/quarterstaff›, these bonuses apply to only one of its ends. If a *flame blade* gains the *frost* or *shock* special ability, the base damage of the spell changes to match the energy type; neither a *flame blade* nor *shillelagh* can gain both of these special abilities. A green scourge cannot add the *throwing* property to a *flame blade* unless she also adds the *returning* property." alter="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Aberration Sense (Ex)]{l=1 icon=skill-boost passive="A green scourge adds ‹skill/Knowledge (dungeoneering)› (INT) to her class skills and gains a +2 bonus on Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks." replace="Nature sense"}

::ab[Scentless (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A green scourge cannot be detected or tracked with the ‹umr/scent› ability or any other special senses that rely on acute smell. She can choose to exude a scent if desired." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Resist Unnatural Influence (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A green scourge gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against aberrations' spell-like abilities, supernatural abilities, and poisons." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

halcyon_druid.name: Halcyon Druid
description: ## Halcyon Druid
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/121›

Druids of the Halcyon Circle embrace [the teachings of Old-Mage Jatembe](rule/aff_i_school), combining them with traditional druidic practices. Though they revere the natural world, halcyon druids are less focused on emulating its inhabitants. Instead, like Old-Mage Jatembe, they treat with beings from the Outer Sphere. As a sign of their allegiance, these druids wear fantastical masks depicting celestials, fiends, and (most often) the bestial agathions.

Halcyon druids are peacekeepers, mediating between people and nature, people and the spirit world, and different groups of people. Yet when necessary, they use their magic to fight enemies of peace, especially demons and demon cultists.

Most halcyon druids serve Nantambu and the surrounding villages - making, memorizing, and arbitrating the unwritten pacts between them. Some halcyon druids, however, are drawn farther afield. They may do so to forge new connections between tribes and villages, to spread the message of peace, or to fight demonic outbreaks.

::ab[Bonded Mask (Su)]{icon=magic ability="A halcyon druid forms a powerful bond with a mask, which functions identically to a ‹class/wizard«'s› *bonded object* except that it can be used to cast druid spells (including those gained from class abilities) instead of wizard spells. A *bonded mask* must be worn to have an effect, and it occupies the ‹rule/head slot›. A halcyon druid can enhance her mask with abilities appropriate for a head slot item, and can designate an existing head slot item as her *bonded mask* (but only if it covers her face)." replace="Nature bond" head}
:::ab{next flavor="Text from the wizard class" title="Bonded Object" icon=magic action=Ability}

If you select a bonded object, you begin play with one at no cost. These objects are always masterwork quality. If you attempt to cast a spell without your bonded object worn, you must make a ‹rule/concentration› check or lose the spell. The DC for this check is equal to 20 + the spell's level.

A bonded object can be used once per day to cast any one spell that you have in your spellbook and are capable of casting, even if the spell is not prepared. This spell is treated like any other spell cast by you, including casting time, duration, and other effects dependent on your level. This spell cannot be modified by metamagic feats or other abilities. The bonded object cannot be used to cast spells from your opposition schools.

You can add additional magic abilities to your bonded object as if you have the required item creation feats and if you meet the level prerequisites of the feat. For example, a wizard with a bonded dagger must be at least 5th level to add magic abilities to the dagger (see ‹feat/Craft Magic Arms and Armor›). The magic properties of a bonded object, including any magic abilities added to the object, only function for the wizard who owns it. If a bonded object's owner dies, or the item is replaced, the object reverts to being an ordinary masterwork item of the appropriate type.

If a bonded object is damaged, it is restored to full hit points the next time you prepare your spells. If the object of an arcane bond is lost or destroyed, it can be replaced after 1 week in a special ritual that costs 200 gp per wizard level plus the cost of the masterwork item. This ritual takes 8 hours to complete. Items replaced in this way do not possess any of the additional enchantments of the previous bonded item. You can designate an existing magic item as your bonded item. This functions in the same way as replacing a lost or destroyed item except that the new magic item retains its abilities while gaining the benefits and drawbacks of becoming a bonded item.

::ab[Magaambya-Trained (Ex)]{icon=skill passive="A halcyon druid adds ‹skill/Diplomacy› (CHA) and all ‹skill/Knowledge› (INT) skills to her list of class skills." replace="Nature sense" alter="Class skills"}
::ab[Peacekeeper (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A halcyon druid adds ‹misc/half› her class level (minimum 1) to Diplomacy and Knowledge (local) checks." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Spontaneous Casting]{icon=learn-magic ability="A halcyon druid adds all spells from the ‹domain/Good› cleric domain to her druid spell list, and she can focus stored spell energy into spells from the Good domain that she hasn't prepared ahead of time. She can lose a prepared spell to cast any spell of the same level or lower from the Good domain." replace="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Natural Arcana (Su)]{l=4 icon=learn choice="A halcyon druid chooses two spells from the ‹main/spells_>wizard›/‹main/spells_>sorcerer› spell list and adds them to her druid spell list.
