The chosen spells must be at least 1 level lower than the highest level spell she can currently cast." imp6="She adds two more bonus spells, following the same rules and restrictions above." repeat="She gains two more bonus spells.~8~2~18" imp20="She gains two more bonus spells, but they can be of any level." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Resist Fiendish Influence (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A halcyon druid gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of outsiders with the evil subtype and spells with the evil descriptor." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Embody Mask (Sp)]{l=13 icon=magic standard="A halcyon druid wearing her *bonded mask* can embody the spirit it represents, which can be any Tiny to Large ‹family/agathion›, ‹family/angel›, ‹family/archon›, or ‹family/azata›. This ability functions as per ‹spell/beast shape IV› (using the adjustments for magical beasts), but adds the following abilities if the assumed form has them: *aura of menace, protective aura, speak with animals, telepathy,* and *truespeech.* If the assumed form has ‹umr/immunity› to a condition, the druid gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against effects that cause that condition." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 replace="A thousand faces"}

jungle_druid.name: Jungle Druid
description: ## Jungle Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/100›

The fecund jungles of the equatorial regions are rich in life and ancient tradition; druidical guardians of sacred pools, elder trees, and trembling volcanoes watch over crumbling temples and the inevitable reclamation of lost civilizations by the beating heart of nature untamed.

::ab[Jungle Guardian (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-power passive="A jungle druid gains a bonus on Initiative checks and Climb, Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level in jungle terrain, and she cannot be tracked in such environments." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Woodland Stride (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A jungle druid gains *woodland stride* at this level." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Torrid Endurance (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A jungle druid ignores the effects of a hot climate as if under the effects of ‹spell/endure elements›. She also gains a +4 bonus on saves against disease and the exceptional abilities of animals and magical beasts." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{icon=down l=6 ability="A jungle druid gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for this ability is equal to her druid level - 2." alter="Wild shape"}
::ab[Verdant Sentinel (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power-magic ability="A jungle druid can cast ‹spell/tree shape› at will." replace="A thousand faces"}

kraken_caller.name: Kraken Caller
description: ## Kraken Caller
‹SOURCE Dirty Tactics Toolbox/16›
Some druids draw upon power irreverently stolen from one of the most dangerous creatures of the depths: the kraken.
::ab[Call of the Waves (Ex)]{icon=warning note="A kraken caller must choose ‹faith/Besmara› as her deity. If she ever changes her deity, she loses this archetype and becomes a normal druid."}
::ab[Bonus Languages]{icon=info note="A kraken caller's bonus language options include Aquan instead of Sylvan." alter="Bonus languages"}
::ab[Dauntless Swimmer (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A kraken caller can always take 10 on Swim checks, even if she is rushed or threatened." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Hold Breath (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A kraken caller can ‹misc/hold her breath› for a number of minutes equal to her Constitution score before she risks drowning." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Resist the Waves' Lure (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A kraken caller gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of creatures with the aquatic subtype." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

:::ab{title="Wild Shape (Su)" action=Ability l=4 icon=down-magic-melee alter="Wild shape" imp6="A kraken caller can grow one additional tentacle." imp8="A krakan caller can grow two additional tentacles. As a @HLmove_action, she can retract or regrow any number of the additional tentacles, redistributing the mass of any retracted tentacles. For every 2 retracted tentacles, she can increase the reach of the remaining tentacles by 5 feet (to a maximum of 15 feet), or increase the damage dealt by her tentacles as if she were ‹misc/one size category larger›." imp10="A kraken caller can grow three additional tentacles." imp12="A kraken caller can grow four additional tentacles."}

A kraken caller's options for *wild shape* are limited to animals with the aquatic or water subtype or that have the amphibious special quality. The kraken caller can alternatively expend a daily use of *wild shape* to grow two tentacles out of her body while otherwise retaining her own form. This effect lasts for 10 minutes per level, or until the kraken caller changes back.

The tentacles have a reach of 5 feet regardless of the kraken caller's size, and the kraken caller can use them as secondary ‹umr/natural attacks› that deal 1d4 points of damage (1d3 for a Small kraken caller). She also gains a racial bonus on Climb, Swim, and ‹rule/dirty trick› combat maneuver checks equal to the number of tentacles grown.

While in this form, a kraken caller can expel a 10-foot-radius cloud of ink once per minute as a @HL[standard action]. This cloud provides ‹rule/total »concealment› in water and persists for 1 round per level. The ink has no effect out of water.

::ab[Beast of the Depths (Su)]{l=13 icon=power passive="A kraken caller gains a swim speed equal to her base land speed and can breathe water as though affected by the spell ‹spell/water breathing›." replace="A thousand faces"}

leshy_warden.name: Leshy Warden
description: ## Leshy Warden
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/47;Familiar Folio/7›

The natural world is full of bodiless nature spirits connected to the forces of glades, springs, and individual plants. Some druids hear their call keenly and are able to effortlessly incarnate them as the miniature creatures known as leshys. The leshy warden serves as these spirits' ally, defender, and voice.

::ab[Leshy Familiar (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A leshy warden forms an intimate bond with a nature spirit, incarnating the spirit as a ‹monster/leaf leshy›. She gains a leaf leshy as a ‹sidekick/familiar› and treats her druid level as her effective ‹class/wizard› level for the purpose of this ability. If the leshy dies, the leshy warden can incarnate the same spirit again by paying the normal cost to replace a familiar. So long as the leshy lives, the leshy warden gains access to the ‹domain/Plant› domain as if through a druid's *nature bond* class feature, but she can't choose the Decay subdomain." replace="Nature bond"}

::ab[Green Empathy (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A leshy warden can improve the attitude of a plant creature as if using *wild empathy.* The typical wild plant creature has a starting attitude of indifferent. If the plant creature is mindless, the leshy warden imparts a modicum of intellect to the plant so that it can be interacted with as if it were an animal. A leshy warden can also use this ability to influence an animal, but she takes a -4 penalty on the check to do so." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Leshy Summoner (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A leshy warden is an expert at summoning and growing ‹family/leshy«s›. She counts as a plant creature for the purpose of growing leshys. She adds leaf leshys, ‹monster/gourd leshy«s›, ‹monster/fungus leshy«s›, ‹monster/seaweed leshy«s›, and ‹monster/lotus leshy«s› to her list of creatures she can summon with ‹spell/summon nature's ally I«, II, III, IV, and V›, respectively." alter="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Leshy Tender (Ex)]{icon=boost-magic l4="A leshy warden can either grant her leaf leshy a +2 increase to Strength and Dexterity or transform it into a gourd leshy." l8="The leshy warden can either grant her current leshy a +2 increase to Strength and Dexterity or transform it into a fungus leshy." l12="She can either grant her current leshy a final +2 increase to Strength and Dexterity or transform it into a seaweed leshy." special="If the leshy is transformed, it gains the normal ability scores of its new form; ability score increases granted by this ability don't carry over to its new form." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost passive="A leshy warden gains *wild shape,* except her effective druid level for the ability is equal to her druid level - 2 for the purpose of determining the number of times per day she can use it. The leshy warden can assume the form of only Small or Medium plant creatures, as per ‹spell/plant shape I›." imp8="She can take the form of a Large plant creature, as per ‹spell/plant shape II›." imp10="She can take the form of a Huge plant creature, as per ‹spell/plant shape III›." alter="Wild shape"}

::ab[Plant Whisperer (Su)]{l=13 icon=power flavor="A leshy warden's connection to the spirits of nature becomes strong enough that she can always hear them whispering." passive="She is treated as if constantly under the effects of ‹spell/speak with plants›." ability="Once per day, she can spend 10 minutes in communion with the spirits to learn the answers to her questions, as ‹spell/commune with nature›." order=pa replace="A thousand faces"}

life_channeler.name: Life Channeler
description: ## Life Channeler
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/50›

Life channelers take the natural cycle to what they believe is its logical conclusion. By sacrificing the life of a sentient creature, they can generate energy that guarantees fertility and prosperity for lower creatures like plants and animals. These druids preach to flocks who become willing sacrifices, each serving as a "king" or "queen" for a short time after being chosen as the next sacrifice.

::ab[Alignment]{icon=warning note="In addition to a druid's usual alignment requirements, a life channeler must be of a nongood alignment. If she becomes good, she can no longer take levels in the druid class or fill her *wicker talismans* (see below), but she otherwise keeps all her abilities." alter="Alignment requirements"}

::ab[Plant Preservation (Su)]{icon=power choice="A life channeler must choose the ‹domain/Plant› domain with her *nature bond* (she can select the Growth subdomain if she wishes)." ability="When she performs a ‹misc/coup de grace› that kills a sentient humanoid creature (not a summoned creature), she can do so ritualistically in order to affect the area centered on the body with the enrichment version of ‹spell/plant growth›. The sacrifice is a death effect." order=ca alter="Nature bond"}

:::ab{l=4 icon=magic-power action=Ability title="Wicker Talismans (Su)" imp6="A life channeler gathers a flock of willing sacrifices. This grants her the benefits of the ‹feat/Leadership› feat, except that using a follower as a human sacrifice with this ability doesn't cause the life channeler to take a penalty to her Leadership score." replace="Wild shape"}

A life channeler gains a selection of wicker talismans that can store and convert the life energy of sentient creatures into the growth, health, and fertility of plants around her. The life channeler gains one wicker talisman corresponding to each level of druid spells she can cast, starting with 1st-level spells. These talismans are initially empty, but can hold life energy.

When the life channeler ritualistically kills a creature with a *coup de grace,* she can store life energy in a talisman instead of gaining the *plant growth* effect. The creature must be of a CR equal to at least twice the spell level of the corresponding talisman. For instance, a CR 8 creature's life energy could provide energy for a talisman holding up to a 4th-level spell.

A talisman is either full or empty; it can't hold more than one creature's life energy. A life channeler can expend the energy stored in a talisman to cast her domain spell of the talisman's level without expending a spell slot.

::ab[Rampant Growth]{icon=boost l4="A life channeler can enrich the land more than usual with her sacrifices. When ritually killing a creature with a *coup de grace* for the *plant growth* enrichment effect or casting *plant growth* for the enrichment effect from one of her talismans, the effect has a range of 1 mile instead of a range of half a mile." l8="If the sacrificed creature was at least CR 8, the effect lasts for 3 years instead of 1 year." l12="If the sacrificed creature was at least CR 12, the effect increases productivity to two-thirds above normal, instead of one-third above normal." special="These effects stack, assuming the creature is high enough CR."}

::ab[Stored Life]{l=9 icon=def ability="A life channeler gains a third option when using a *coup de grace* on a sentient humanoid. If the victim had a CR at least as high as the life channeler's class level, the life channeler can store the creature's life energy in her body. She can have the life energy from only 1 creature in her body at a time. While she is storing life energy, if she would take damage or die from a death effect or if she would gain negative levels or take energy drain, she can sacrifice the stored energy to negate the effect without spending an action." replace="Venom immunity"}

lion_shaman.name: Lion Shaman
description: ## Lion Shaman
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/103›
A shaman with this totem calls upon the proud lion, imposing and majestic, the mighty leader of deadly hunters.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A lion shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a ‹companion/lion›. If choosing a domain, the lion shaman must choose from the ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Glory›, ‹domain/Nobility›, and ‹domain/Sun› domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A lion shaman can use *wild empathy* with felines as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
A lion shaman may adopt an aspect of the lion while retaining her normal form. The druid may select from the following bonuses:
- **Movement** (+20 enhancement bonus to land speed)
- **Senses** (low-light vision, ‹umr/scent›)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (bite [1d4], 2 claws [1d4] for a Medium druid, rake, +2 CMB to grapple)
While using *totem transformation,* the lion shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (felines only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="A lion shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon felines, and these summoned creatures gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to any feline to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates."}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost passive="A lion shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of a feline, she instead uses her druid level + 2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="A lion shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Lunge›, ‹feat/Improved Iron Will›, ‹feat/Iron Will›, or ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Acrobatics)." imp13="The druid gains another feat from the list above."  imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

mantella.name: Mantella
description: ## Mantella
‹SOURCE Potions and Poisons/4›
A mantella focuses on exploring her poisonous heritage, whether or not it has manifested in her own personal biology.
::prereq{r=Grippli}

::ab[Poison Affinity (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost-down note="If a form the mantella assumes with *wild shape* has any poison abilities, the mantella also gains those abilities. However, the mantilla can never use *wild shape* to take the form of an elemental." alter="Wild shape"}

::ab[Toxic Diet (Ex)]{l=6 icon=def passive="Both the mantella and her animal companion gain immunity to poison." immediate="When exposed to a dose of poison, a mantella can absorb the toxin and store it internally for up to 1 day." swift="A mantella can secrete this poison; this changes the poison's type to injury and applies the dose of poison to all of the druid's natural attacks." order=piwu useInc=druid~2~6~3 useF="6~3~2" alter="Venom immunity" replace="Spontaneous casting"}

menhir_savant.name: Menhir Savant
description: ## Menhir Savant
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/38›
Some druids study the paths of nature's power through the nodes and ley lines that connect standing stones and megalithic circles, learning to tap into their energies.

::ab[Spirit Sense (Sp)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A menhir savant can detect the presence of undead; fey; outsiders; and astral, ethereal, or incorporeal creatures. This ability functions like ‹spell/detect undead›, and the druid detects all of these creatures rather than trying to detect one kind." replace="Nature sense~wild empathy"}

::ab[Place Magic (Su)]{l=2 icon=boost flavor="A menhir savant learns to identify and tap into ley lines in different types of terrain." free="She can tap into the magic of a nearby ley line and increase her caster level by +1 for 1 round." useMod=Wis3 replace="Woodland stride~trackless step"}
::ab[Walk the Lines (Su)]{l=9 icon=magic ability="A menhir savant can use her connection to ley lines to cast ‹spell/transport via plants›." useMod=Wis}
::ab[Empty Body (Su)]{l=13 icon=magic standard="A menhir savant can become ethereal, as if using ‹spell/ethereal jaunt›." useL=druid useNC useUnit=round replace="A thousand faces"}

mooncaller.name: Mooncaller
description: ## Mooncaller
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/38›
A mooncaller is bound to the subtle influences of the ever-changing moon and its endless cycles from light to dark and back again.

::ab[Night Sight (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A mooncaller gains low-light vision. If she already has low-light vision, she gains darkvision with a range of 30 feet. If she already has darkvision, the range of her darkvision is increased by 30 feet." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Resist Call of the Wild (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A mooncaller gains a +4 bonus on saving throws to avoid confusion, daze, feeblemind, and insanity effects. She also gains a +4 bonus against the exceptional, spell-like, and supernatural abilities of creatures with the shapechanger subtype." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Purity of Body (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="A mooncaller gains immunity to all diseases, including supernatural and magical diseases." replace="Venom immunity"}
::ab[Wolfsbane (Su)]{icon=def l13="A mooncaller gains DR 3/silver." l16="This becomes DR 4/silver." l19="This becomes DR 5/silver." replace="A thousand faces"}

mountain_druid.name: Mountain Druid
description: ## Mountain Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/100›
As more and more of the soft, easy lands become cultivated and civilized, many druids look for refuge and solitude among the eternal peaks of the highest mountains.

::ab[Mountaineer (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-power passive="A mountain druid gains a bonus on Initiative checks and Climb, Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level in mountainous terrain, and she cannot be tracked in such an environment." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Sure-Footed (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A mountain druid suffers no penalty to speed or on Acrobatics or Stealth checks when walking across steep slopes, rubble, or scree." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Spire Walker (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost-def passive="A mountain druid does not lose her Dexterity bonus when climbing. A mountain druid is immune to ‹rule/altitude sickness› and ignores the effects of a cold climate as if under the effects of ‹spell/endure elements›." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{icon=down-boost l=6 ability="A mountain druid gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for this ability is equal to her druid level - 2. A mountain druid cannot use *wild shape* to adopt a plant form." alter="Wild shape"  imp12="She can assume the form of a Large giant as if using ‹spell/giant form I›." imp16="She may assume the form of a Huge giant as if using ‹spell/giant form II›."}

::ab[Mountain Stance (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="A mountain druid gains immunity to petrification and receives a +4 bonus on saving throws or to CMD to resist any attempt to push, pull, bull rush, or drag her, or to resist any other effect that would physically move her from her position (e.g., ‹spell/repel wood›, ‹spell/reverse gravity›, or being blown away by high winds). This does not protect her against being tripped, grappled, or overrun." replace="Venom immunity"}

::ab[Mountain Stone (Ex)]{l=13 icon=power-magic ability="A mountain druid can transform her body into a weathered stone outcrop and back at will. This effect functions as ‹spell/statue›." replace="A thousand faces"}
